About: It is about a coastal commune in the Seine-Maritime department, Normandy, northern France.
Affects: It affects the inhabitants of the town.
AssociatedWith: It is associated with Camille Saint-Saëns.
BelongsTo: It belongs to the Pays de Caux.
BuiltIn: It was built after 1696 in a typical French classical style.
FoundedIn: It was founded in 1030 as a small fishing settlement.
HasA: It has a regular ferry service to Newhaven in England.
It has a popular pebbled beach.
It has the churches of Saint-Jacques and Saint-Remi.
It has a 15th-century castle.
It has a museum and exhibition space.
It has a strong maritime collection.
It has a rich collection of 17th- and 18th-century ivory carvings.
It has a monument erected by the town commemorating the long relationship between it and Canada.
It has a remarkable architecture.
It has an outdoor seawater swimming pool.
HasFeatureOf: It is characterized by its unique feature for a sea port.
InfluencedBy: It was influenced by the 1824 visit of the widowed Duchess of Berry.
IsA: It is a city in New Brunswick, Canada.
KnownAs: It is known as Dieppois (m) and Dieppoise (f) for its inhabitants.
KnownFor: It is known for its scallops.
It is known for lacy folding fans.
LocatedIn: It is located in the Seine-Maritime department.
It is located in Normandy, northern France.
It is located on the Channel coast.
It is located north of Rouen.
It is located at the mouth of the river Arques.
It is located east of the mouth of the river Scie.
It is located in the commune of Saint-Aubin-sur-Scie.
MemberOf: It is a member of the Alabaster Coast.
OccurredAt: It occurred at the mouth of the river Arques.
It occurred at the mouth of the river Scie.
OccurredDuring: It occurred during the Hundred Years' War.
It occurred during the Second World War.
It occurred during the fall of France in 1940.
It occurred during the Dieppe Raid.
It occurred during the Battle of Normandy.
OccurredIn: It occurred in northern France.
It occurred in the 16th century.
It occurred in the 17th century.
It occurred in South Carolina.
It occurred in New France.
It occurred in England.
It occurred in Geneva.
It occurred in Scotland.
It occurred in the New World.
It occurred in 1940.
PartOf: It is part of the Seine-Maritime department.
It is part of the English Channel.
It is part of the Pays de Caux.
It is part of the Alabaster Coast.
It is part of the region of Normandy.
UsedAs: It is used as a museum and exhibition space.
It is used as a seaport.
It is used as a seaside resort.
UsedFor: It is used for fishing techniques.
It is used for presenting the Upper Normandy coast.
It is used for the electricity and telecommunications networks.
UsedIn: It is used in 2022 for the electricity and telecommunications networks.
It is used in the 16th century.
UsedTo: It is used to bring water from a source to the city.
